# Assumes an ssl cert for your domain, and assumes its location
#     generated by letsencypt; certbot should place everything neatly
# Assumes a webapp run on port 8000 and API run on 5000
# Assumes an example domain name, you can change it in vim like so
#     %s/webappsubdomain.example.com/yourdomainhere/g


# Redirect HTTP to HTTPS
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name webappsubdomain.example.com;

    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name webappsubdomain.example.com;

    # SSL configuration (managed by Certbot)
    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/webappsubdomain.example.com/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/webappsubdomain.example.com/privkey.pem;
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    ssl_dhparam /etc/letsencrypt/ssl-dhparams.pem;

    # Proxy requests to Gunicorn for the web app
    location / {
	# First block bots that spam our logs
        if ($http_user_agent ~* (AhrefsBot|SemrushBot|MJ12bot|Baiduspider|zgrab|Keydrop)) {
            return 403;
        }

        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8000;  # Web app bound to this address
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }

    # Proxy requests to the API (under /api/)
    location /api/ {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:5000;  # API bound to this address
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }

    # Optional: Serve static files directly for the web app
    location /static/ {
        alias /var/www/webappsubdomain.example.com/src/webapp/static/;
    }

    # Optional: Add error pages for a polished user experience
    error_page 404 /404.html;
    location = /404.html {
        root /var/www/webappsubdomain.example.com;
    }
}
